steps.title: Spot the contrast
steps.content: The sentence contrasts “some locations” with “other areas with insufficient supply,” where insufficient supply means too few bicycles. The blank must describe the opposite condition for those other locations.

steps.title: Match the supply condition
steps.content: ”Saturated with” means overstocked or filled to capacity—the word that captures how one-way trips create an imbalance, leaving some locations with excess bicycles and others with shortages. The other choices don't work: “susceptible to” and “contingent on” are contextually inappropriate, and “depleted of” would repeat the insufficient-supply problem rather than show the contrast. That matches choice C — C is correct.
